title: Pet Care Supplies - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Basic Needs > Pet Care Supplies 8 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF COMMUNITY ACTION RESOURCE ENTERPRISES INC EMERGENCY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with utility bills, one-time rent payments, and prescriptions. May have personal items such as clothes, hygiene products, diapers, and pet supplies, depending on donations received. 2310 1st Street Suite 2 Tillamook, OR 97141 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm (503) 842-5261 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COUNCIL ON AGING OF CENTRAL OREGON PET PALS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides pet food, pet supplies, and limited basic veterinary care assistance. 1036 NE 5th Street Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Meals on Wheels participants Hours: Pet food deliveries: Varies Phone: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (541) 678-9770 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FIDO PET FOOD BANK PET NEEDS PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides pet care supplies. 14186 Fir Street Suite 203 Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 3rd Saturday of the month 9am-2pm Email (503) 655-3663 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEHALEM BAY COMMUNITY SERVICES NEHALEM BAY FOOD AND CLOTHING PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a pantry with food, cleaning, and hygiene items. Also operates a clothing bank for both adults and children, with new and used clothes, a limited amount of household goods, and medical supplies (such as walkers). Animal Haven is also housed in the pantry offering animal food and other items for pet care. Offers lunches to school students during the summer and school holidays. 36050 10th Street Nehalem, OR 97131 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday/Friday/Saturday 10am-2pm, Wednesday 1pm-5pm Email (503) 368-5612 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NO PET LEFT BEHIND PET ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides financial assistance with necessary veterinary procedures. The amount will depend on available funding. Also provides pet supplies such as food, coats, and other items depending on availability. Eligibility: Individuals with low or no income Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 778-6328 Get Directions More Details PET HELP FINDER PET RESOURCES LOCATOR Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Locator for veterinary services, pet food pantry, supplies, pet services such as boarding, co-housing shelters, pet care for seniors, transportation support, and behavioral help. Locator also lists grant programs, free and discounted services, and pop-up events such as vaccine clinics. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Not applicable Email Visit Website More Details PORTLAND ANIMAL WELFARE TEAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides veterinary health care clinics by appointment and access to a pet supply bank. Households can access the pet supply bank monthly without appointments or pre-qualification. Veterinary care is offered by appointments or walk-in pre-scheduled drop-in clinic dates. Dates of drop-in clinics are available on website and phone voicemail. Information can also be provided in person during regular hours. Does not offer emergency, urgent, or walk-in veterinary care outside of pre-scheduled drop-in clinic dates. At veterinary clinics, maximum of two pets per household will be seen. Services include: -Basic health exams -Vaccines -Flea and de-worming treatments -Pet supplies -Discounted animal licensing Accepts donations of new and gently used pet supplies and some medications. Check website for specific donation requirements. Cannot accept: Any food past one year of expiration, CBD treats or supplements, retractable leashes, shock collars, electric collars, or prong collars. Volunteer opportunities are available. 1718 NE 82nd Avenue Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: Pets of people who are experiencing homelessness or extreme poverty. Monthly income must be below the federal poverty level. Pets must be spayed/neutered or client must be willing to spay/neuter their pet. County Licensing: Clackamas, Multnomah, and Washington County residents Volunteers: Age 16 and older Hours: Tuesday-Thursday 10am-3pm Veterinary care: By appointment only Vaccine and Wellness Clinics: Varies Email (503) 206-6033 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ROGUE VALLEY STREET DOGS PET CARE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides spay and neuter services, pet gear such as collars, harnesses, and bowls, pet food, and financial assistance for urgent veterinary care when available. Does not cover vet bills already accrued. Volunteer opportunities available and accepts donations of pet food, supplies, and gear as well as financial donations. Eligibility: Low-income households or homeless individuals Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 816-6035 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ACTION RESOURCE ENTERPRISES INC EMERGENCY SERVICES COUNCIL ON AGING OF CENTRAL OREGON PET PALS FIDO PET FOOD BANK PET NEEDS PANTRY NEHALEM BAY COMMUNITY SERVICES NEHALEM BAY FOOD AND CLOTHING PANTRY PORTLAND ANIMAL WELFARE TEAM PORTLAND ANIMAL WELFARE TEAM
